title: 10 possible side effects of Chamomilla recutita (German Chamomile) if used improperly
intro: Chamomilla recutita, also known as German chamomile, is a plant that has been used for medicinal purposes for centuries. When used improperly, it can cause several health side effects. First, it can lead to allergic reactions, resulting in skin rashes, hives, and swelling of the face, tongue, or throat. Additionally, it may cause digestive issues such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged use or high doses can result in uterine contractions, making it unsuitable for pregnant women. Furthermore, it may interact with certain medications, including blood thinners and sedatives, leading to adverse effects. Lastly, it's worth noting that the steam distilled essential oil of Chamomilla recutita contains the compound bisabolol, which can cause eye irritation and conjunctivitis if used near the eyes.
